description: Design project structure, architecture, and detailed development phases for the Unreal Engine autonomous AI agent. Combines Claude Code + MCP for bidirectional UE communication. TDD methodology throughout.

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding & Monorepo Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the monorepo directory structure initialized with all config files so that both the MCP server (Node.js/TS) and UE plugin can coexist in one repository.
acceptanceCriteria: Root contains: package.json (workspace), .gitignore, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md
mcp-server/ initialized with package.json, tsconfig.json (strict), vitest.config.ts
mcp-server/src/ has index.ts, server.ts, and subdirectories: transport/, tools/, state/, rag/, observability/, types/
mcp-server/tests/ has unit/, integration/, fixtures/ subdirectories
ue-plugin/ has UnrealMasterAgent.uplugin descriptor
ue-plugin/Source/UnrealMasterAgent/ has Build.cs, Public/, Private/ directories
ue-plugin/Source/UnrealMasterAgentTests/ has Build.cs for test module
docs/ has slate-templates/, api-reference/, coding-conventions/, schemas/ subdirectories
scripts/ has dev-start.sh, test-all.sh
.claude/mcp.json template created
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (tsc --noEmit)
npm install succeeds

userStories.title: Architecture Decision Records
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive architecture documentation so that all technical decisions, data flows, and interface contracts are recorded for consistent implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: ARCHITECTURE.md documents 4-layer architecture with diagrams (text-based)
WebSocket protocol schema defined in docs/schemas/ws-protocol.schema.json
Blueprint AST JSON schema defined in docs/schemas/blueprint-ast.schema.json
MCP tool manifest schema in docs/schemas/tool-manifest.schema.json
Error code taxonomy documented (1000-6099 ranges)
Threading model documented (GameThread dispatch requirement)
Key decision: reverse WS connection direction (UE as client, Node.js as server) documented with rationale
Key decision: in-memory LRU caching (not Redis) documented with rationale
Key decision: minimize Python, prefer C++ for UE-internal ops documented
All AGENTS.md files updated to reflect architecture
Documents are accurate and internally consistent

userStories.title: MCP Server Core Types & Message Codec
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TypeScript type definitions and message encoding/decoding so that all communication between layers is type-safe and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types/messages.ts defines WSMessage and WSResponse interfaces
src/types/ws-protocol.ts defines WebSocket envelope types
src/types/blueprint-schema.ts defines Blueprint AST TypeScript types matching JSON schema
src/types/compile-result.ts defines compilation result types
src/transport/message-codec.ts implements encode/decode with Zod validation
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/transport/message-codec.test.ts written before implementation
Tests cover: round-trip encode/decode, malformed input rejection, all message types
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: MCP Server Skeleton with stdio Transport
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the MCP server to boot via stdio transport and register with Claude Code so that tool discovery works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts creates McpServer with StdioServerTransport
src/server.ts wires tool registry
src/observability/logger.ts redirects ALL console.log to stderr (stdout is JSON-RPC only)
src/tools/registry.ts provides tool registration framework
src/tools/tool-schemas.ts has Zod schemas for initial tools
TESTS FIRST: test that logger blocks stdout, test that registry registers tools
Server starts via 'node dist/index.js' without errors
.claude/mcp.json configured correctly
Claude Code discovers server and lists available tools
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: WebSocket Bridge & Connection Manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the MCP server to maintain a persistent WebSocket connection to the UE plugin with automatic reconnection so that communication is reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/transport/websocket-bridge.ts implements WS server (Node.js listens, UE connects as client)
src/transport/connection-manager.ts implements exponential backoff reconnection (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s)
Request/response correlation via Map<string, {resolve, reject, timeout}>
30-second timeout per request with proper cleanup
Heartbeat mechanism to detect stale connections
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/transport/websocket-bridge.test.ts with mock client
Tests cover: connect, send/receive, timeout, reconnection on drop, heartbeat
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: UE Plugin Module & WebSocket Client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Unreal Engine plugin to load in the editor, connect to the MCP bridge as a WebSocket client, and route messages to handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: UnrealMasterAgent.uplugin configured as Editor-only module
UnrealMasterAgent.Build.cs references required UE modules (WebSockets, Json, JsonUtilities, EditorSubsystem)
FUnrealMasterAgentModule starts WS client on module startup, stops on shutdown
UMAWebSocketServer.h/.cpp (renamed to UMAWebSocketClient) connects to Node.js WS server
UMAMessageHandler.h/.cpp routes incoming messages by 'method' field to handlers
UMAMessageTypes.h defines C++ structs matching ws-protocol schema
All WS message handlers dispatch to GameThread via AsyncTask(ENamedThreads::GameThread)
TESTS FIRST: UMAWebSocketServerTest.cpp (client connects, sends, receives, handles malformed JSON)
Plugin loads in UE 5.x editor without errors
All UE automation tests pass

userStories.title: E2E Ping/Pong Verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete end-to-end ping tool so that the full Claude Code → MCP Server → WebSocket → UE Plugin → Response pipeline is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tool 'editor.ping' registered in tool registry
Tool sends ping via WS bridge, UE plugin responds with pong
TESTS FIRST: tests/integration/mcp-tool-roundtrip.test.ts with mock UE client
scripts/dev-start.sh starts MCP server in dev mode
Full E2E test: Claude Code calls 'editor.ping', receives 'pong' response
All tests pass
TypeScript compiles with zero errors

userStories.title: Editor Query Tools (Level, Actors, Assets)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Claude to query UE editor state (current level, actors, asset info) so that the agent has situational awareness.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools: editor.getLevelInfo, editor.listActors, editor.getAssetInfo
UMAEditorQueries.h/.cpp implements C++ handlers for each query
UMAEditorSubsystem.h/.cpp provides persistent editor-lifetime object for state
Results returned as structured JSON matching defined schemas
TESTS FIRST: unit tests for each tool handler, UE automation tests for each query
Claude can ask 'what level is open?', 'list actors', 'describe asset /Game/X'
All tests pass

userStories.title: Blueprint JSON AST Schema & Fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Blueprint AST JSON schema finalized with test fixtures so that serialization/deserialization has a verified contract.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/schemas/blueprint-ast.schema.json is complete and valid JSON Schema
Schema covers: blueprintPath, blueprintClass, parentClass, graphs[], nodes[], pins[], linkedTo[]
Pin types use short strings (int, float, bool, exec, object:ClassName)
src/types/blueprint-schema.ts TypeScript types match JSON schema exactly
tests/fixtures/sample-blueprint.json is a realistic sample conforming to schema
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/tools/blueprint.test.ts validates sample against Zod schema
Schema supports partial serialization (single node, single graph)
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Blueprint Serializer (C++)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the UE plugin to traverse UEdGraph and serialize Blueprints to the defined JSON AST format so that Claude can read Blueprint structures.
acceptanceCriteria: UMABlueprintSerializer.h/.cpp traverses UBlueprint->UbergraphPages
Iterates Graph->Nodes and Node->Pins with full type information
Outputs JSON matching blueprint-ast.schema.json exactly
Handles EventGraph type initially (AnimGraph, MacroGraph deferred)
Pin types encoded as short strings from FEdGraphPinType
TESTS FIRST: UMABlueprintSerializerTest.cpp loads BP_TestActor, serializes, validates field counts
Test Blueprint fixture (BP_TestActor) created in Content/Tests/
All UE automation tests pass

userStories.title: Blueprint Node Spawning & Pin Connection (C++)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the UE plugin to programmatically spawn Blueprint nodes and connect pins so that Claude can modify Blueprints.
acceptanceCriteria: UMABlueprintManipulator.h/.cpp spawns nodes via FKismetEditorUtilities
Spawned nodes get AllocateDefaultPins() called and are added to graph
Pin connections use UEdGraphSchema_K2::TryCreateConnection (NOT raw MakeLinkTo)
Incompatible pin types rejected with descriptive error messages
FBlueprintEditorUtils::MarkBlueprintAsModified() called after modifications
TESTS FIRST: UMABlueprintManipulatorTest.cpp - spawn node assert exists, link pins assert LinkedTo
All UE automation tests pass

userStories.title: Blueprint MCP Tools & Stateful Caching
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MCP tools for Blueprint operations with server-side caching so that Claude can serialize, create nodes, connect pins, and modify properties without token waste.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools: blueprint.serialize, blueprint.createNode, blueprint.connectPins, blueprint.modifyProperty, blueprint.deleteNode
src/state/cache-store.ts implements LRU cache (configurable max entries, 60s TTL)
Large Blueprint JSON cached server-side; only cache key + summary returned to Claude
Subsequent operations reference cache key for full data
TESTS FIRST: cache-store.test.ts (LRU eviction, TTL, hit/miss), blueprint tool handler tests
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Slate Template RAG System
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a local RAG system for Slate UI templates so that Claude gets relevant code examples injected into context when generating Slate widgets.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/slate-templates/ contains: base-widget.md, list-view.md, tree-view.md, details-panel.md, toolbar.md, dialog.md, tab-widget.md
Each template follows Epic C++ coding conventions with SNew/SLATE_BEGIN_ARGS patterns
src/rag/embedding-store.ts generates embeddings via @xenova/transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
src/rag/semantic-search.ts implements vector similarity search
src/rag/slate-templates.ts loads and indexes templates
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/rag/semantic-search.test.ts - 'create a list view' returns list-view.md with >0.8 relevance
Embeddings stored in flat JSON file (corpus < 100 docs)
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Slate Widget Generation & Validation Tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Claude to generate syntactically valid Slate C++ code with pre-validation so that LLM output errors are caught before compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: src/tools/slate/generate-widget.ts uses RAG templates to augment generation context
src/tools/slate/validate-slate.ts pre-validates: balanced SNew brackets, SLATE_BEGIN/END_ARGS matching, TAttribute usage
Style guidelines enforced via system prompt injection
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/tools/slate.test.ts - template retrieval, validation catches common errors
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: File Operations with Safety Gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Claude to read/write source files in the UE project with human approval for destructive operations so that the agent can generate code safely.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools: file.read, file.write, file.search
UMAFileOperations.h/.cpp implements file ops with path bounds checking
src/state/safety.ts classifies operations as safe/destructive
Destructive writes (overwrite existing, modify production BPs) trigger human-in-the-loop approval
Path traversal attacks blocked
60-second approval timeout defaults to reject
TESTS FIRST: safety.test.ts (destructive op detection, approval flow, timeout), file ops unit tests
tests/integration/safety-gate.test.ts for full approval flow
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Live Coding Controller & Compile Log Parser (C++)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the UE plugin to programmatically trigger Live Coding and parse compile output into structured errors so that the agent can compile and understand errors.
acceptanceCriteria: UMALiveCodingController.h/.cpp wraps ILiveCodingModule
Guarded with #if WITH_LIVE_CODING
Checks IsEnabledForSession() before Compile()
Binds to GetOnPatchCompleteDelegate() for completion callback
Fallback documented for macOS/Linux (full UnrealBuildTool compile)
UMACompileLogParser.h/.cpp hooks FOutputDevice to capture compile log
Parses MSVC/Clang error format into {file, line, column, severity, message, code}
Handles multi-line errors and template instantiation backtraces
TESTS FIRST: UMALiveCodingControllerTest.cpp, UMACompileLogParserTest.cpp with sample error strings
All UE automation tests pass

userStories.title: Compilation MCP Tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MCP tools for triggering compilation, polling status, and retrieving parsed errors so that Claude can compile and read results.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools: compilation.trigger, compilation.getStatus, compilation.getErrors
Compile trigger sends command to UE plugin, returns immediately with status ID
Status polling returns: pending, compiling, success, failed
Error retrieval returns structured parse results from UMACompileLogParser
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/tools/compilation.test.ts
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Self-Healing Compile Loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the agent to autonomously detect compile errors, read relevant source, fix code, and recompile so that compilation errors are self-correcting.
acceptanceCriteria: Self-healing relies on Claude Code's reasoning to chain tool calls (NOT server-side state machine)
MCP server provides compilation.selfHeal meta-tool that orchestrates: getErrors → file.read → fix → file.write → compilation.trigger
Hard cap at 3 retry loops before requesting human help
Retry count tracked per session
TESTS FIRST: integration test with intentional compile error fixture
Given compile error, agent: reads error, reads source, generates fix, writes fix, recompiles, verifies success
All tests pass

userStories.title: Observability & Tracing Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every tool call traced to LangSmith/Langfuse so that agent behavior can be monitored, debugged, and optimized.
acceptanceCriteria: src/observability/tracer.ts emits OpenTelemetry-compatible trace spans
Each trace includes: tool name, duration_ms, success/failure, input summary, error details
src/observability/metrics.ts tracks tool call latency and success rates
Integration with LangSmith or Langfuse API
TESTS FIRST: unit test for trace emission format
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Dynamic Tool Registry (Micro-Tools via RAG)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tools dynamically registered based on conversation context so that the context window is not bloated with all 30+ tool schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Core tools (10-15) registered statically at boot
Additional tools discovered via RAG semantic search on conversation context
Server emits notifications/tools/list_changed when tool set changes
src/tools/registry.ts supports dynamic add/remove
TESTS FIRST: tests/unit/rag/semantic-search.test.ts for tool retrieval accuracy
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Human-in-the-Loop Safety System (Full)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete safety system with in-editor approval dialogs so that destructive agent operations require explicit developer confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: UMAApprovalGate.h/.cpp spawns Slate dialog in UE editor for approval
Operations classified: safe (queries, reads), warn (writes to new files), dangerous (overwrites, deletes, modifies production BPs)
Dangerous operations blocked until approval received
60-second timeout = reject
Approval/rejection sent back through WS to MCP server to Claude
TESTS FIRST: UMAApprovalGate automation test, safety-gate integration test
All tests pass

userStories.title: In-Editor Chat Panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dockable chat panel inside the UE editor so that I can interact with the agent without switching to a terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom Slate widget creates a dockable tab in UE editor
WebBrowser component renders chat interface
Messages routed to Claude Code, responses displayed in panel
Panel registered via UMAEditorSubsystem
TESTS FIRST: UMAEditorSubsystem test for panel registration
All UE automation tests pass

userStories.title: Documentation & AGENTS.md Hierarchy Update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all documentation (AGENTS.md, README, ARCHITECTURE) updated at each phase completion so that AI agents and developers always have accurate project context.
acceptanceCriteria: Root AGENTS.md updated with actual (not planned) directory structure
AGENTS.md files created for: mcp-server/, ue-plugin/, docs/, tests/ and their subdirectories
All AGENTS.md have correct <!-- Parent: --> references
README.md has setup instructions, development workflow, TDD guidelines
ARCHITECTURE.md matches actual implementation decisions
RAG docs (docs/) have accurate Slate templates and API references
Documentation is internally consistent and up-to-date
